mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.9% as of Oct 2022 indicates prior easing; threshold tests tightening regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, which equity investors price as negative earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146063 thousand as of Oct 2022 provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite negative payroll signal, or signal fails to predict negative returns with directional accuracy > 52% over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in real retail sales and housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, indicating reduced future corporate revenues. This interaction effect produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both series decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 474948M and HOUST at 1215K as of Sep 2022 establish baseline for joint deterioration
regime_hint: demand-side recession with synchronized consumer and housing weakness
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in industrial production index below 103.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining corporate earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.0998 as of Sep 2022 indicates proximity to threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances when INDPRO < 103.0, or signal exhibits Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration of consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.8%) combined with elevated federal funds rate signals persistent inflation expectations and central bank commitment to tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as real discount rates remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 244.158 and FEDFUNDS at 0.9% as of Oct 2022
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% signaling labor market slack and reduced consumer spending power, which equity investors interpret as earnings headwind, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% as of Oct 2022 is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 54% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown and reduced corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings multiples downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.0% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16842.4B as of Jul 2022 provides baseline for growth calculation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 53% of instances during sub-2% GDP growth periods, or signal exhibits Rank IC < 0.07 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1200 thousand units while real retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month, indicating sustained consumer demand and residential construction activity that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1215K and RSAFS at 474948M as of Sep 2022 indicate strength
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained demand momentum
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.
